title: Add an Image to A Form
excerpt: How can I add an image to a form, Not a background image?

posts.author: mimi (Mimi Fishman)
posts.cookedHtml: How can I add an image to a form, Not a background image?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @mimi,

To clarify are you interested in uploading an image to a form when you’re submitting the form itself (via the files column)? Or are you interested in uploading an image to a question/form descriptions?

posts.author: Nachliel (Nachliel from Easyapps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @mimi

You can add images to the form description as well as to the description of the fields in the form using the HTML tag <img src="LINK_TO_IMAGE">.

Here is an example using a monday form:

monday form with image1182×1500 122 KB

However our monday app Easyform supports advance styling features helping you better display your image.

The Easyform equivalent of the form above (with the additional <center> tag that is not supported in the monday form) will look like this:

easyfrom with image1449×1513 183 KB

So if you are interested in creating styled forms that better match your brand and embed perfectly in you web page you can check out our monday.com app Easyform and our extensive documentation site.

You can set up a free demo here or contact us at support@easyappshq.com.

Here is another example of our form in action:

I hope this helps

Nachliel

posts.author: DavidSchenkler (David Schenkler | monday ambassador cohort 7)
posts.cookedHtml:
You can do it here!

Embedding images to Workform descriptions Special workflows & use cases
Ever wanted to embed an image directly into your question description? Here’s how you can do it! Step 1: Host your image First, upload your image to Postimages (or another image-hosting site). Choose any expiration time you want Select your preferred image size After uploading, copy the Direct Link (not the thumbnail or page link) Step 2: Add the HTML to your question In your question description, insert the following HTML: <img src="PASTE_DIRECT_LINK_HERE"> …
